methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Anthropic is a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Dario and Daniela Amodei and others, having raised $13B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

The peer-median method is the primary triangulation approach applicable here. The median valuation across these four comparables is $19.5B, with a range spanning $7.0B to $300.0B. This wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity within frontier AI companies—OpenAI's valuation sits materially above the group median, while Groq anchors the lower bound. The median better represents a central tendency than the extreme outliers in this set.

The triangulated valuation range derived from this analysis is $16.6B to $22.4B, centered at $19.5B median. This implies the current stated valuation of $183B sits approximately 9.4x the peer median. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given rapid stage migration in frontier AI, and the comparability of business model maturity and revenue generation across these companies. The wide peer range ($7.0B–$300.0B) itself signals material disagreement in the market regarding appropriate valuations for this sector and stage.
